title: Koordersiodendron pinnatum
uses: Koordersiodendron pinnatum is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. Its bark is believed to possess antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating various infections and wounds. In traditional medicine, the plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Additionally, Koordersiodendron pinnatum is valued for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe sore throats and relieve joint pain. The plant's medicinal uses have been passed down through generations in these regions, where it is often used in combination with other herbs to create effective remedies.
taxonomy: Koordersiodendron pinnatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant is native to Southeast Asia and is classified as an evergreen tree. The classification of Koordersiodendron pinnatum places it within the order Malvales and the clade flowering plant. It is further categorized as a member of the family Dipterocarpaceae, which is a group of tropical trees known as the "iron trees." This classification reflects the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species.
